surface.name: Wise Business personal API token + cURL GET /v1/profiles
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation presents this as the hosted path from account setup to Call GET /v1/profiles. It reaches the requested first-success boundary without extending into post-success production work.

entry_point.developer_intent: Wise accepts a Business personal API token and returns the authenticated account's profiles from GET /v1/profiles.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no prior Wise Platform setup for this journey.
The journey starts at account creation and stops at the first documented successful outcome.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Wise accepts a Business personal API token and returns the authenticated account's profiles from GET /v1/profiles.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The API returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of Wise profiles.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official documentation leads the developer to: Call GET /v1/profiles.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable happy-path result on the selected Wise Business personal API token + cURL GET /v1/profiles surface; later configuration and production hardening occur after success.

prerequisites.requirement: A Wise Business account eligible to create a personal API token.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to the Wise Business account used for API access.
primary_path.input: A new developer starting at account creation
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Create a personal API token in the documented Wise settings flow and copy it securely.
primary_path.output: A personal API token is available.
primary_path.success_signal: A personal API token is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Send the documented authenticated cURL GET request to /v1/profiles with the personal token.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Confirm the response is HTTP 200 and contains the account's profile objects.
primary_path.output: A profiles JSON array is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: A profiles JSON array is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

friction_gates.description: Personal API tokens are for eligible Wise Business customers and are not the partner OAuth path.
The bearer token must be protected because it grants access to the associated Wise account.

sources.title: Call GET /v1/profiles
evidence_supported: Wise accepts a Business personal API token and returns the authenticated account's profiles from GET /v1/profiles.
The API returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of Wise profiles.

uncertainties.question: Does the current fresh-account UI exactly match every navigation label in the documented Call GET /v1/profiles path?
uncertainties.impact: Navigation wording may differ while the required outcome and boundary remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation establishes the journey but account-specific UI state can vary.
